title: Atlas Stream Processing Monitoring and Alerting

sections.heading: Monitor Stream Processor Workspaces in the Atlas UI
sections.body: For each of your stream processing workspaces you can monitor your stream processors in the Atlas UI:

sections.heading: Step 1: Select the stream processing workspace you want to monitor.
sections.body:
In the pane of the stream processing workspace you want to monitor, click
**Manage**.

sections.heading: Step 2: Click the  tab.
sections.body:
The **Monitoring** tab displays a variety of runtime
statistics about a stream processor of your choosing, including, but
not limited to:

- Number of messages ingested

- Number of messages successfully processed

- Number of messages sent to your [Dead Letter Queue](atlas-sp-dlq)

If your source connection is [Apache Kafka](https://kafka.apache.org/), you can monitor the
lag between the current offset and the latest offset at the broker for a topic's
partition and the sum of all the partition lags.

sections.heading: Step 3: Filter the monitoring charts as needed.
sections.body: You can filter the charts by stream processor name, time range, and granularity.

sections.heading: Stream Processor Monitoring Methods
sections.body:
Atlas Stream Processing provides the following methods for on-demand reporting
about your stream processors:

- [sp.processor.sample()](manual/reference/method/sp.processor.sample)

- [sp.processor.stats()](manual/reference/method/sp.processor.stats)

The `sp.processor.sample()` method allows you to see a small sample of
the documents output by a currently running stream processor of your
choosing. Users can compare the sampled results against their expected
results to diagnose any errors in their aggregation pipeline design.

The `sp.processor.stats()` method returns a variety of runtime
statistics about a stream processor of your choosing, including, but
not limited to:

- Number of messages ingested

- Number of messages successfully processed

- Number of messages sent to your [Dead Letter Queue](atlas-sp-dlq)

- In-memory size of your pipeline state

- Pipeline definition

If your source connection is [Apache Kafka](https://kafka.apache.org/), you can monitor the
following optional metrics:

- `partitionOffsetLag` indicates the lag between the
  current offset and the latest offset at the broker for a topic's
  partition.

- `kafkaTotalOffsetLag` indicates the sum of all the partition lags.

sections.heading: Stream Processing Logs
sections.body:
Atlas Stream Processing provides two types of stream processing workspace activity logs:

- **Operational logs**

  , which primarily track the behavior of

  individual stream processors.

- **Audit logs**

  , which primarily track authentication and security

  activity at the stream processing workspace level.

To download Atlas Stream Processing operational or audit logs:

sections.heading: Step 1
sections.body:
Navigate to the pane of the stream processing workspace from which to
download logs and click the ellipsis.

sections.heading: Step 2: In the dropdown menu, select .
Step 3
sections.body:
In the modal window, select the type of log you want to
download.

sections.heading: Step 4
sections.body:
In the **Stream processor** field, provide the
name of the stream processor for which you want to download
logs. Leave this field blank to download logs for all stream
processors.

sections.heading: Step 5
sections.body:
From the **Time Period** dropdown, select the
interval for which you want to download logs.

sections.heading: Step 6: Click the  button.
sections.body: > **Note:** Logs take up to five minutes after creation to become queryable.

sections.heading: Stream Processor Metrics in Datadog
sections.body:
You can send metrics to Datadog to monitor your stream
processors. To learn how to configure the integration and
which metrics are available, see [Datadog](datadog-integration).

sections.heading: Stream Processor Alerts
sections.body:
Atlas Stream Processing triggers alerts when processors change state, or a
processor meets various ingestion or output thresholds. For a
list of available Atlas Stream Processing alerts, see [Atlas Stream Processing Alerts](atlas-sp-alerts). To
learn more about alert configuration, see [Configure Alert Settings](configure-alerts).

You can target Atlas Stream Processing alerts in the following ways:

- All stream processors within a project

- All stream processors within a stream processing workspace matching the configured
  predicate

- All stream processors the names of which match the configured
  predicate

For targets other than all stream processors, you can configure
multiple targets for the same alert.

Atlas Stream Processing configures a [Stream Processor State is failed](<Stream Processor State is failed>) alert by default. As this is a
project-level alert, it applies to any stream processor running in any
stream processing workspace within the project for which it is configured.
